message:
feat(tantivy_ingest): Cx-first compute_indexer_lag (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on to ticks 52/53. Adds the Cx-first lag snapshot entry
point and factors the snapshot construction into a pure helper
shared with the legacy path.

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `compute_indexer_lag_with_cx(cx, storage, consumer_id)` —
    Cx-first parallel of `compute_indexer_lag`. Checkpoints at
    both hot seams:
      - pre-flight: a pre-cancelled cx returns before
        `storage.health()` runs.
      - between the two storage reads: a cx cancelled during the
        health read skips the redundant `read_checkpoint` when
        the result would be discarded.
    Cancellation errors are mapped onto `RecorderStorageError::Io`
    via `std::io::Error::other(...)` so the function signature
    stays identical to the legacy version — callers can swap
    `_with_cx` in without touching error handling.

  * `build_indexer_lag_snapshot(&health, checkpoint)` — pure
    helper extracted from the legacy `compute_indexer_lag`. Both
    variants feed their (health, checkpoint) pair through this
    helper so they produce bit-for-bit identical snapshots for
    the same inputs. Records_behind + caught_up computation lives
    exactly once.

Regression test (new) `compute_indexer_lag_with_cx_matches_legacy`:

  * Drives parity checks across three interesting states:
    1. Empty storage (no events, no checkpoint). Both should
       return a caught_up snapshot with zero counts.
    2. Populated but never indexed (3 events, no checkpoint).
       Both should report 3 records_behind, caught_up=false.
    3. Partially indexed (2 events indexed out of 3). Both should
       report the same checkpoint ordinal and same records_behind.
  * Asserts ALL four snapshot fields match between legacy and
    cx-first at every state transition.

If a future refactor of `build_indexer_lag_snapshot` changed the
never-indexed arm semantics (e.g. `head + 1` vs `head`), both
variants would break together — the parity test guards against
divergence but not against shared bugs. Existing legacy tests
(`lag_monitor_no_events`, `lag_monitor_with_events_no_checkpoint`,
`lag_monitor_partially_indexed`, `lag_monitor_fully_caught_up`)
cover the absolute semantics.

With tick 54, `tantivy_ingest.rs` now has Cx-first siblings for all
three public async entry points:
  * `run` → `run_with_cx` (tick 53)
  * `run_with_reader` → `run_with_reader_with_cx` (tick 52)
  * `compute_indexer_lag` → `compute_indexer_lag_with_cx` (tick 54)

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * tantivy_ingest:: — 123 passed / 0 failed (tick 53: 122 -> tick
    54: 123, +1 new parity test; all 4 legacy lag_monitor tests
    still green, proving the snapshot-helper extraction preserved
    semantics)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
